principles: Use block.json as the canonical registration source on PHP and JavaScript sides.
Store agent-editable content in declared attributes or InnerBlocks, never only in generated markup.
Prefer semantic registered blocks over arbitrary HTML.
Render important public content on the server so it exists in initial HTML.
Use theme.json presets and controlled enums instead of arbitrary CSS values.

assetFields.style: registered shared handle or file: path

blockJson.registration: register_block_type() must receive the block directory so metadata and assets are discovered from block.json

requirements: Editor code must work inside an iframe and must not depend on styles or globals from the parent admin document.
Use useBlockProps() on the editor root and get_block_wrapper_attributes() on the server root.
Register editor and front-end assets explicitly through block metadata.
Do not query or mutate editor DOM outside the block tree.
Use WordPress packages declared in index.asset.php instead of undeclared globals.

rules: Every editable value must be declared in block.json.
Use enum for layouts, tones, alignment, spacing and visual variants.
URLs are strings but must be escaped with esc_url() at render time.
Limit repeaters in render.php with array_slice() and document limits in ai.json.
Avoid attributes containing raw HTML, CSS, JavaScript, PHP or serialized executable code.
Blocks with visible headings should expose headingSize as a controlled enum (auto, xs, sm, md, lg, xl) and map it through styleContract rather than arbitrary CSS values.

dynamicBlocks.save: return null, except container blocks may save InnerBlocks.Content

supports.color: prefer theme tokens or controlled tone attributes
supports.typography: avoid uncontrolled per-instance settings for agent-built pages

rules: Declare shared design tokens once in a namespace-wide selector that automatically matches future aipilot blocks; never maintain an enumerated list of block wrapper classes.
Every block that uses shared tokens must load the shared front-end style handle and the shared editor style handle through block.json.
Every CSS custom property must include a safe fallback to a theme preset or literal value so the block remains usable with another compatible theme.
A visual attribute must use a typed enum and must map to a documented CSS class, CSS variable, or server-rendered branch in ai.json styleContract.
The editor preview and render.php must use the same attribute values and class naming contract.
Every non-base enum value must have an explicit CSS selector; base values may rely on the root selector only when declared in styleContract.baseValues.
Use get_block_wrapper_attributes() and useBlockProps() so the generated wp-block-aipilot-* wrapper class is always present on front end and in the iframe editor.
After adding or changing a block, run the authoring audit and manually switch every visual option in both editor and front-end preview.

aiJsonStyleContract.requiredFor: Every new or modified block with CSS-driven attributes.

fields.rootClass: Stable CSS class placed on the rendered block root.
fields.tokenScope: Use shared when the block consumes --ap-* variables.

requiredBeforeRelease: All required block files exist and contain valid JSON/PHP/JavaScript.
block.json uses apiVersion 3, supports.html=false, and declares editorScript, style, editorStyle and render.
Shared token selector is namespace-wide and includes future block wrappers automatically.
Every declared styleContract matches block.json enums, render.php classes or variables, and CSS selectors.
Every background/tone option is tested in the editor iframe and on the public page.
REST authoring audit returns valid=true with no errors.
qualityGates.failurePolicy: Do not publish or package a block library while authoring audit contains errors.

page.h1: exactly one primary H1; aipilot/hero provides H1

rules: Do not select heading levels for visual size; style headings with CSS or theme.json.
Every titled section should use aria-labelledby pointing to its visible heading.
Use native lists, buttons, links, details and summary before adding ARIA roles.

requirements: Interactive controls must have an accessible name and keyboard operation.
Decorative shapes, symbols and repeated visual-only labels must use aria-hidden=true.
Informative images require meaningful alt text; decorative images use empty alt.
Links must have valid href values and describe their destination outside surrounding context.
Use aria-expanded, aria-controls and focus management only for custom interactive widgets; native details/summary does not need duplicated roles.
Dynamic status updates in the editor should use WordPress a11y announcements when user feedback is required.
Respect prefers-reduced-motion and never make motion essential to understanding.
Keep important text visible in server-rendered HTML.
Use one descriptive H1 and a logical heading hierarchy.
Expose public pages through WordPress sitemap and internal links.
Structured data must match visible page content.
Do not hide keyword text in images, CSS pseudo-elements or aria-label attributes.
llms.txt and public JSON context are discovery aids, not replacements for crawlable HTML and sitemap.
Claims about SEO or AI discoverability must reflect active site capabilities.

forbidden: arbitrary style attributes outside allowed supports

page.h1Policy: Exactly one H1 inside page content or template. Hero may provide it.

post.h1Policy: No H1 inside post_content; single-post template provides the H1.

headings: The single-post template owns H1. Generated post content must begin with a lead or paragraph, then use H2 for main sections and H3 for nested sections.
Do not use heading level for visual sizing.
Inside post content, keep vertical heading rhythm compact: H2-H4 should introduce the next paragraph without landing-page-sized gaps.
links: Set newTab only when it benefits the reader; render rel="noopener noreferrer" with target="_blank".
Use link-card for important contextual links and article-links for source lists.
images: Every informative image requires alt text describing purpose, not appearance only.
Captions provide source or context and do not replace alt text.
Do not place essential text only inside images.
quotes: Use pullquote for editorial quotations inside posts and testimonial for full-width marketing sections.
archives: All archive queries must inherit the current category, tag, author, date or search context.
Cards use semantic article wrappers and linked post titles.
widths: Single-post headers, featured images, article content, article blocks, author boxes, related posts and comments use the shared 1024px editorial column.
CTA is the only article block allowed to use a full-viewport background; its inner content remains aligned to the 1024px editorial column.
Do not use arbitrary per-block widths inside post content.
Full-bleed CTA backgrounds must use a symmetric bleed technique that cannot leave a right-side strip or trigger horizontal scrolling.
faq: FAQ headingSize controls the section heading and questionSize controls summary questions independently.
Use compact vertical padding inside posts; landing pages may use the default roomier section spacing.

validationContexts.page: Page content; exactly one H1 provider expected.
validationContexts.post: Post content only; H1 is forbidden because the single-post template owns it.
validationContexts.template: Generic FSE template; one H1 expected.
validationContexts.single-post-template: Requires one core/post-title at level 1 and one core/post-content.
validationContexts.archive-template: Requires one H1 and at least one core/query with query.inherit=true.

aiPilotSystemArticle.title: Что такое AI Pilot: как ИИ помогает собирать и развивать WordPress-сайт
goals: Представить систему AI Pilot в понятной форме.
Показать, как связаны тема, блоки, коннектор и AI-интерфейс.
Использовать большую часть редакционных блоков в одной статье.
Соблюдать требования SEO и AI-search readiness.

seo.title: Что такое AI Pilot: ИИ-система для WordPress и FSE
seo.metaDescription: Разбираем, что такое AI Pilot, из каких компонентов состоит система и как она помогает создавать WordPress-сайты и статьи с учетом SEO и AI-поиска.

editorialLayout.readingWidth: 900px desktop target; may reduce to 760px on medium viewports
editorialLayout.fullWidth: Full-width sections must not be placed inside an outer group whose contentSize caps alignfull or alignwide children
editorialLayout.templateRule: Place core/post-content directly in the single template with layout.contentSize and layout.wideSize. Do not wrap it in a narrower constrained group.
editorialLayout.blockRule: Editorial blocks must set width:100%, max-width:none, min-width:0 and box-sizing:border-box; the parent FSE alignment controls their final width.
editorialLayout.duplicateRule: author-box and related-posts may be provided either in post content or by the template fallback, but must render only once.
